action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 13 trained a zero-init ResidualAdapter for 3 epochs, but val PESQ stayed exactly at 2.7755 (the pretrained baseline) while loss hovered around 2.33 with almost no change. This strongly suggests the adapter parameters were not actually being optimized (likely an optimizer bug in the truncated train.py). The pretrained MetricGAN+ still holds the best test PESQ of 3.134. Analysis: Before abandoning the adapter idea, I must verify that the optimizer explicitly receives adapter.parameters(), use a combined loss (L1 + MR-STFT), and confirm that weight norms update during the first steps. If the adapter truly learns but still fails to improve val PESQ, I'll revert to the pretrained checkpoint. Decision: Rewrite train.py with an unambiguous optimizer on adapter.parameters(), higher LR (5e-4), gradient clipping, ReduceLROnPlateau, and combined time+freq loss. Keep model.py the same but update predict.py to load adapter config from checkpoint. Run a 10-epoch training with full validation each epoch. If val PESQ improves, continue; otherwise submit the pretrained baseline.
